source.anchorText: subtitle: A minimal persistent advisor lane
source.endAnchorText: subtitle: A minimal persistent advisor lane

items.text: This pinned card acts like a tiny advisor, Circle, or standing organization surface. It stays available from the hand and can be replaced with the user's own persistent actor.

source.anchorText: This pinned card acts like a tiny advisor, Circle, or standing organization

items.text: The advisor helps the organization turn a loose idea into a modest first step.

source.anchorText: The advisor helps the organization turn a loose idea into a modest first step.
source.endAnchorText: The advisor helps the organization turn a loose idea into a modest first step.

items.text: The advisor remains available while the organization keeps listening.

source.anchorText: The advisor remains available while the organization keeps listening.
source.endAnchorText: The advisor remains available while the organization keeps listening.

items.text: This card is intentionally small. It demonstrates the action-card lane without copying an existing game system.

source.anchorText: This card is intentionally small. It demonstrates the action-card lane without
subtitle: Convert capacity into visible progress.
source.endAnchorText: subtitle: Convert capacity into visible progress.

items.text: The organization spends a small amount of capacity to move the project forward.

source.anchorText: The organization spends a small amount of capacity to move the project forward.
source.endAnchorText: The organization spends a small amount of capacity to move the project forward.

items.text: The organization keeps its limited capacity available for another moment.

source.anchorText: The organization keeps its limited capacity available for another moment.
source.endAnchorText: The organization keeps its limited capacity available for another moment.

items.text: This paragraph is the event body. In Studio, it is the kind of text players edit when they change an Event or Scene body.

source.anchorText: This paragraph is the event body. In Studio, it is the kind of text players edit
source.endAnchorText: when they change an Event or Scene body.

items.text: The two choices below show how option text becomes a button. Each result writes a small variable so you can see how effects shape later conditions.

source.anchorText: The two choices below show how option text becomes a button. Each result writes
source.endAnchorText: a small variable so you can see how effects shape later conditions.

items.text: This is result text. It appears after the player clicks the option. The effect `demo_support += 1` also unlocks a follow-up option on the starter screen.

source.anchorText: This is result text. It appears after the player clicks the option. The effect
source.endAnchorText: `demo_support += 1` also unlocks a follow-up option on the starter screen.

items.text: This is another result. The effect `demo_conflict += 1` changes the state summary, but it does not unlock the support follow-up.

source.anchorText: This is another result. The effect `demo_conflict += 1` changes the state
source.endAnchorText: summary, but it does not unlock the support follow-up.

items.text: `demo_support` is above 0, so the support line can now appear.

source.anchorText: [? if demo_support > 0 : `demo_support` is above 0, so the support line can now appear. ?]
source.endAnchorText: [? if demo_support > 0 : `demo_support` is above 0, so the support line can now appear. ?]

items.text: `demo_conflict` is above 0, so the observation line can now appear.

source.endAnchorText: [? if demo_conflict > 0 : `demo_conflict` is above 0, so the observation line can now appear. ?]

items.text: If neither line appears yet, choose one of the options in the demo event first.

source.anchorText: If neither line appears yet, choose one of the options in the demo event first.
source.endAnchorText: If neither line appears yet, choose one of the options in the demo event first.

items.text: This section is hidden until `demo_support` is above 0. In Studio, conditions like this appear as `view-if` or requirements. They explain why some player choices are not visible at the start.

source.anchorText: This section is hidden until `demo_support` is above 0. In Studio, conditions
source.endAnchorText: choices are not visible at the start.

items.text: This is a whiteboard hand scene. It gives the project a ready-made card surface: one deck lane for action cards and one pinned advisor lane.

source.anchorText: This is a whiteboard hand scene. It gives the project a ready-made card surface:
source.endAnchorText: one deck lane for action cards and one pinned advisor lane.

items.text: In a larger mod, this can become the place where monthly actions, party work, advisors, circles, or other repeatable systems live.

source.anchorText: In a larger mod, this can become the place where monthly actions, party work,
source.endAnchorText: advisors, circles, or other repeatable systems live.

items.text: This bundled project is a safe place to learn how a Dendry mod is built. The next scene acts like a tiny in-game event: it has a title, a body, two choices, result text, and effects that change simple variables.

source.anchorText: This bundled project is a safe place to learn how a Dendry mod is built. The
source.endAnchorText: result text, and effects that change simple variables.

items.text: In Studio, open Explore or Design after loading this template. Select **A Small Campaign Office** to see how the source-backed text maps to the player-facing event. Select **Open the workspace hand** to see the minimal hand/deck/advisor skeleton used by card-style DendryNexus projects.

source.anchorText: In Studio, open Explore or Design after loading this template. Select
source.endAnchorText: hand/deck/advisor skeleton used by card-style DendryNexus projects.

items.text: This sidebar belongs to the bundled demo template. It gives a whiteboard version of the common SDAAH-style dashboard: simple resources, support, card progress, advisor trust, and room for more status sections.

source.endAnchorText: advisor trust, and room for more status sections.

items.text: This bundled project is a safe place to learn how a Dendry mod is built. The
next scene acts like a tiny in-game event: it has a title, a body, two choices,
result text, and effects that change simple variables.
In Studio, open Explore or Design after loading this template. Select
**A Small Campaign Office** to see how the source-backed text maps to the
player-facing event. Select **Open the workspace hand** to see the minimal
hand/deck/advisor skeleton used by card-style DendryNexus projects.

diagnostics.message: post_event.scene.dry is intentionally opaque; only bounded targeted routing/news summary is indexed.
